title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
description: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.

title: Can orbital data centers help justify a massive valuation for SpaceX?
description: On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we debated Elon Musk's vision for data centers in space.

title: In Japan, the robot isn’t coming for your job; it’s filling the one nobody wants
description: Driven by labor shortages, Japan is pushing physical AI from pilot projects into real-world deployment.

title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
description: It’s about to become more expensive for Claude Code subscribers to use Anthropic’s coding assistant with OpenClaw and other third-party tools.

title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
description: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's looming IPO poised to reshape the landscape for everyone.

title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
description: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
description: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
description: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
description: A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
description: Moonbounce has raised $12 million to grow its AI control engine that converts content moderation policies into consistent, predictable AI behavior.

title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
description: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.

title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
description: MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.

title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
description: Google is adding a way to customize and instruct avatars for video creation in the Vids app.

title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
description: The firm says it can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
description: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.

title: Anthropic is having a month
description: A human really borks things at Anthropic for the second time this week.
